originalTitle: France returns sacred 'talking drum' looted during colonial rule to Ivory Coast
title: France returns sacred 'talking drum' looted during colonial rule to Ivory Coast
content: The move forms part of a broader process of returning cultural artefacts to African countries that started in 2017.‎

originalTitle: Ghana star to deny rape charges, UK court hears
title: A Famous Footballer and Important Grown-Up Rules
content:
A Famous Footballer from Ghana

Hello, my amazing friends all over Africa! It’s your kid-friendly news reporter, here to share something important. We are talking about a very famous footballer. Do you know who Thomas Partey is? He is a super player from our own Ghana! He plays football with lots of skill and makes Ghana very proud. He also plays for a big team far away in a country called the United Kingdom, or the UK. Many of you might have seen him play on TV or heard about his amazing goals. He is a true star on the football field! Did you know? Ghana is a country in West Africa, known for its beautiful beaches, tasty jollof rice, and of course, its fantastic football players! Football is like a super big family game across Africa, bringing everyone together.

What Are These Big Grown-Up Rules About?

Now, sometimes, big grown-ups have big discussions about important rules. It’s like when you have rules at home or at school to make sure everyone is safe and happy. Sometimes, someone might say that another person did something very wrong. This is called making an “accusation.” It means someone is pointing a finger and saying, “Hey, you did this bad thing!” Recently, some serious questions have been asked about our star footballer, Thomas Partey. Someone has made a very serious accusation against him. This happened a while ago, in December 2020. Imagine it like someone saying you took their toy, but you remember you didn't! It’s a very serious situation for grown-ups. Did you know? Rules are very important for everyone. They help us know what is right and wrong and keep everyone safe. Just like in football, there are rules to make sure the game is fair!

A Special Place Called Court

When very serious accusations are made, grown-ups go to a special place. This place is called a “court.” Think of it like a very important meeting room. In this room, there are special grown-ups called “judges” and “lawyers.” Judges are like the head teachers of the court; they make sure everything is fair and follows the big rule books. Lawyers help people tell their side of the story. This special court for Thomas Partey is not in Ghana. It is far away in the UK. He will have to go to this court very soon, in the coming weeks. It’s a place where they try to find out the truth about what happened. It’s like when you and your friend have a disagreement, and an adult helps you both talk about it to understand what happened. Did you know? Judges wear special robes in court, which are like long, fancy dresses. It helps everyone know they are the important person who listens to everything carefully.

Saying "I Didn't Do It"

The news tells us something very important about Thomas Partey. It says that he is going to tell the court, “I didn't do it!” This means he is saying that the accusation is not true. He is denying the charges. Imagine if someone said you ate the last cookie, but you know you didn't. You would say, “No, it wasn't me!” That’s what “denying” means. So, in this court, the judge and the lawyers will listen to the person who made the accusation. And they will also listen to Thomas Partey, who says he did not do anything wrong. Everyone gets a chance to tell their story. This is a very important part of being fair. Did you know? In court, people promise to tell the truth. It's a very serious promise, because finding the truth is super important for fairness.

Why Is This Important to Know?

You might be wondering why we are talking about this. Well, it’s important for a few reasons. First, Thomas Partey is a famous person from our home, Africa. When something big happens to someone famous, people want to know. Second, it teaches us about how big grown-up rules work. Even if you are a big football star, you still have to follow rules, and if someone makes an accusation, you have to talk about it in court. It's also important to remember that just because someone is accused of something, it doesn't mean they actually did it. We must wait for the court to listen to everyone and decide what is true. It’s like when you hear a rumor at school; you don't just believe it, right? You wait to find out the real story. Did you know? Fairness means treating everyone the same and giving everyone a chance to speak. It’s like sharing your toys equally with all your friends.

What Happens Next?

So, what will happen now? Thomas Partey will go to the court in the UK. The judge will listen to all the stories and look at all the information. It might take some time, because finding out the truth can be tricky and needs lots of careful listening and thinking. After listening to everyone, the judge will make a decision. This decision will tell everyone what the court believes happened. Until then, it’s important for us all to be patient and wait. We shouldn't guess or make up our minds before the court does. We let the grown-ups who are experts in rules, like the judges, do their important work. Thomas Partey will continue to play football because the court process takes time, and he is innocent until proven otherwise. Did you know? The legal system, with its courts and judges, has been around for thousands of years in different ways! It's how people have tried to be fair and solve big problems for a very long time.

Thinking About Fairness

This whole situation reminds us about how important fairness is in the world. Everyone, no matter how famous or how young, deserves to be treated fairly. Everyone deserves a chance to tell their side of the story. And everyone deserves to have careful grown-ups listen to them before making a big decision. So, while we continue to cheer for our amazing African footballers like Thomas Partey, we also remember the big, important lessons about truth, fairness, and following rules. It's a bit like learning the rules of a game; it helps everyone play well and be happy. We hope that the truth will be found, and fairness will win the day. Did you know? In many African cultures, community elders used to sit together to solve problems and make fair decisions, just like judges do today in courts!
summary: A famous football player from Ghana named Partey is involved in a big grown-up discussion far away in a country called the UK, where serious questions are being asked, and he says he did not do anything wrong.
whyMatters: This is important because it teaches us about big grown-up rules and how we find out what is fair when someone is accused of something, even if they are very famous.
